title: symphyotrichum subspicatum var. grayi
intro: Symphyotrichum subspicatum var. grayi, also known as Gray's Aster or Western American Aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, fever, and sore throats. Herbal preparations of Gray's Aster typically involve drying the flowers and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
